title: Add custom date range selection to PDF Report page

requirements.text: On the PDF Report page (RapportPDFPage.tsx), add a third period selection mode called 'Custom Range' (alongside the existing 'Month' and 'Year' modes). This mode must allow the user to pick a start date and an end date freely (e.g., January 1st to March 31st). The existing table format used for monthly and yearly reports must be reused unchanged for this custom range output.

quotes: je peux choisir par sélection un rapport sur un mois que je choisi ou un rapport sur une année que je choisi. Je voudrai pouvoir choisir par exemple du 1er janvier au 31 mars, en gardant le même tableau

acceptanceCriteria: The report period selector on RapportPDFPage shows three options: By Month, By Year, and Custom Range.
When 'Custom Range' is selected, two date pickers appear: one for start date and one for end date.
The user can set start date to January 1st and end date to March 31st and generate a report covering exactly that period.
The generated report uses the same table format as the existing monthly/yearly reports.
Appointments and financial data are filtered to include only records whose dates fall within the selected start and end dates (inclusive).
The existing 'By Month' and 'By Year' modes continue to work as before without any regression.

requirements.text: Update the data aggregation logic in RapportPDFPage.tsx to support filtering appointments by an arbitrary date range (start timestamp to end timestamp), so that the custom range period feeds into the same report-building pipeline used by month and year modes.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared filtering utility or inline logic accepts a startDate and endDate and returns appointments within that range.
The custom range report totals (revenue, appointments, credits) are computed correctly using the same calculation functions as the existing modes.
The PDF download triggered from custom range mode produces a valid, well-formatted document matching the existing PDF layout.

constraints: Do not modify the existing table format used for PDF reports.
Do not break the existing 'By Month' and 'By Year' report modes.
Do not edit files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Adding new columns or sections to the existing report table.
Backend changes — all filtering can be done client-side using already-fetched appointment data.
Changing the PDF visual design or branding.
